idea-plugin.name: Prompt Bundler
idea-plugin.description:
PromptBundler
Get your codebase into any AI chat, without the copy-paste hell.

Pasting code into a web chat usually means hunting files, copying them one by one, and losing the project structure that makes an answer good. PromptBundler fixes that: pick your files, get one clean, structured prompt, and paste it wherever you want.

🚀 What it does
Attach files, whole folders, or a selected snippet, then ask your question in the tool window. PromptBundler assembles a single, well-structured prompt ready on your clipboard.
  • Seamless Context Gathering: Attach files via the Project view, editor selection, drag-and-drop, or a quick picker.
  • Structured Output: Generates a prompt containing a clean file tree and the exact file contents.
  • Universal Compatibility: Works instantly with ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Mistral, Microsoft 365 Copilot, or any text-based LLM.
  • Smart Filtering: Binary and oversized files are skipped automatically.
  • Consistent & Traceable: Reuses templates for uniformity and keeps a history of past prompts.
🔒 Private by design
No API calls, no accounts, no network. PromptBundler strictly reads selected files and writes plain text to your clipboard. Your code never leaves your machine until you paste it into the chat you or your company approves.

💡 Why bother?
IDE AI agents are capped and getting pricier. Send large context questions to the web subscriptions your company already pays for, and save your limited IDE agent credits for the work that truly needs them.

❤️ Support the project
PromptBundler is completely free, open-source, and runs entirely offline. If it saves you from copy-paste hell, you can support the development here:
Sponsor on GitHub   Buy Me a Coffee

action.description: Attach the selected file(s) or folder(s) to the Prompt Bundler context
Attach the selected text as a snippet to the Prompt Bundler context

action.text: Add Console Output to Prompt Bundler
action.description: Attach the console output (selection or full text) to the Prompt Bundler context
